info.name: MedEx 3.3.0 — Full API Collection
info.description:
# MedEx 3.3.0 — Complete API Collection

**Package:** `com.medex.bd.medex`  
**Version:** 3.3.0 (versionCode 13)  
**Extracted from:** `lib/arm64-v8a/libapp.so` (Flutter AOT), `MainActivity.java`, `res/values/strings.xml`, `AndroidManifest.xml`

---

## Hosts covered
| Host | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `https://medex.com.bd/api/beta/` | Primary MedEx REST API |
| `http://apps.acibd.com/apps/myaci/` | ACIBD order backend (cleartext HTTP) |
| `https://script.google.com/macros/s/…/exec` | Google Apps Script analytics webhook |

---

## Auth flow (call order matters)
1. **POST `/login`** → captures `data.authToken` + `data.userId` into collection variables `auth_token` & `user_id` (via test script).
2. **POST `/verify-otp`** — only required when `data.otpRequired = true` in the login response.
3. **GET `/resend-otp?user_id={{user_id}}`** — resend if OTP expired.
4. All subsequent protected endpoints use `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` (set at collection level).

---

## Collection Variables
| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `base_url` | `https://medex.com.bd/api/beta` | MedEx API base (no trailing slash) |
| `auth_token` | _(empty)_ | Bearer token — auto-set by Login test script |
| `user_id` | _(empty)_ | Numeric user ID — auto-set by Login test script |
| `acibd_base_url` | `http://apps.acibd.com/apps/myaci` | ACIBD base URL |
| `acibd_order_token` | _(empty)_ | ACIBD session token — auto-set by ACIBD Login test script |
| `brand_id` | `1` | Example brand/drug ID for detail requests |
| `generic_id` | `1` | Example generic ID |
| `company_id` | `1` | Example company ID |
| `drug_class_id` | `1` | Example drug-class ID |
| `dosage_form_id` | `1` | Example dosage-form ID |
| `job_id` | `1` | Example job ID |
| `history_id` | `1` | Example history entry ID |

---

## Response envelope (MedEx)
```json
{ "success": true, "message": "...", "data": {} }
```
`data` is an object or array depending on the route.  
Unauthenticated calls to protected routes return `{"success":false,"message":"Authentication token missing","data":[]}`.

variable.description: MedEx primary REST API base URL (no trailing slash)
Bearer token from POST /login → data.authToken. Auto-populated by the Login request test script.
Numeric user ID from POST /login → data.userId. Auto-populated by the Login request test script.
ACIBD order backend base URL (plain HTTP — cleartext)
ACIBD session/order token returned after ACIBD login. Auto-populated by ACIBD Login test script.
Example brand (drug) ID for detail/favorite/history requests
Example generic ID for detail requests

item.name: 🔐 Auth
item.description:
Authentication flow: Login → (Verify OTP if required) → all subsequent calls use the captured Bearer token.

**Call order:**
1. Login — captures `auth_token` + `user_id`
2. Verify OTP — only if `otpRequired = true` in login response
3. Resend OTP — if OTP expired
4. Logout — optional server-side session invalidation

exec: // Auto-capture auth token and user ID into collection variables
console.log('⚠️  OTP required — call "Verify OTP" next with user_id:', res.data.userId);
console.error('Failed to parse login response:', e);

request.description:
**Register or log in** to MedEx.

The server validates all fields server-side; error messages reference field names directly.

### Request body fields
| Field | Type | Constraint | Description |
|-------|------|-----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | min 3 chars | User display name |
| `mobile_number` | string | valid BD mobile | Phone number (used as identifier) |
| `profession` | string \| int | server-defined values (e.g. `"1"`) | User's profession category |

### Response (success)
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Login successful",
  "data": {
    "userId": 45012641530,
    "otpRequired": false,
    "authToken": "<long-hex-token>",
    "displayMessage": "Login successful",
    "otpValidity": 0,
    "resendInterval": 0,
    "loginVerified": false
  }
}
```

**After this call:** The test script auto-captures `auth_token` and `user_id` into collection variables. If `otpRequired = true`, call **Verify OTP** before proceeding.

**Auth:** None (public endpoint)

exec: // If the server issues a new/refreshed token after OTP verification, capture it
console.log('✅ auth_token refreshed after OTP verification');

request.description:
**Verify the OTP** sent to the user's mobile number after login when `otpRequired = true`.

Call this **only when** the Login response returns `"otpRequired": true`.

### Request body fields
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `user_id` | string \| int | User ID from Login response (`data.userId`). Collection variable `{{user_id}}` is pre-set. |
| `otp` | string | 4–6 digit OTP received via SMS |

### Error response (invalid OTP)
```json
{ "success": false, "message": "Invalid attempt.", "data": [] }
```

**Prerequisite:** `POST /login` must be called first to obtain `user_id`.
**Auth:** None (public endpoint)

query.description: Numeric user ID from Login response (auto-set in collection variable)

request.description:
**Resend OTP** to the user's registered mobile number.

### Query parameters
| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `user_id` | integer | ✅ Yes | User ID from Login response. Collection variable `{{user_id}}` is pre-filled. |

### Response (invalid session)
```json
{ "success": false, "message": "Invalid attempt!", "data": [] }
```

**Prerequisite:** `POST /login` must be called first to obtain `user_id`.
**Auth:** None (public endpoint — `user_id` is the identifier)

exec: // Clear local auth token on successful logout
console.log('✅ Logged out — auth_token and user_id cleared');

request.description:
**Invalidate the current session** server-side.

> ⚠️ **Evidence level: INFERRED.** The client symbol `apilogout` exists in `libapp.so` but a naive GET/POST to `/api/beta/logout` returned **404** during live probing. The app may perform **local-only token clearing** without a server call. Try this endpoint and check for 200/404.

### Request body
Empty JSON object `{}` or may need `{"authToken": "{{auth_token}}"}`.

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` (inherited from collection)

item.name: 📄 Static Content
item.description: Public static content endpoints — no authentication required. Returns HTML strings inside the `data` field (rendered in-app via WebView).

request.description:
Retrieve the MedEx **Terms & Conditions** document.

### Response shape
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "...",
  "data": "<html>...</html>"
}
```
`data` is a raw **HTML string** displayed in an in-app WebView — not a JSON object tree.

**Auth:** None (public)

Retrieve the MedEx **Privacy Policy** document.

### Response shape
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "...",
  "data": "<html>...</html>"
}
```
`data` is a raw **HTML string** displayed in an in-app WebView.

**Auth:** None (public)

item.name: 💊 Brands (Drugs)
item.description:
Brand/drug listing and detail endpoints. These map to the `apiDrugsList`, `apiDrugsDetailsList`, and `apiGenericBrandsShortBy` client symbols.

Authentication is optional — unauthenticated calls may return limited data or a token-missing envelope. Full data requires a valid Bearer token.

query.description: Pagination — 1-indexed page number
Sort field (e.g. name, price). Disable to use default ordering.

request.description:
Retrieve a **paginated list of all brands (drugs)**.

**Client symbol:** `apiDrugsList`

### Query parameters
| Param | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `page` | integer | Optional | Page number (1-indexed). Omit for first page. |
| `sortBy` | string | Optional | Sort field — `name`, etc. (from binary string `brands?sortBy=`). |

### Response `data` item fields (example)
```json
{
  "id": 12345,
  "name": "Paracetamol 500mg",
  "strength": "500mg",
  "category": "Analgesic",
  "dosageForm": { "id": 1, "name": "Tablet" },
  "company": { "id": 86, "name": "Square Pharmaceuticals" },
  "generic": { "id": 10, "name": "Paracetamol" },
  "packages": [ { "size": "10s", "price": 12.00 } ]
}
```

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` (optional — but required for full data)

query.description: Filter to generic/unbranded drug entries
Pagination page number

request.description:
List brands filtered to **generic/unbranded (OTC) type** (`type=gen`).

Binary fragment: `brands?type=gen&page=`

**Client symbol:** `apiDrugsList` (with type filter)

### Query parameters
| Param | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `type` | string | `gen` = generic/unbranded |
| `page` | integer | Page number |

**Auth:** Bearer token (optional, inherited from collection)

query.description: Sort field. Known value: name. Other values may include price, company, etc.
Pagination page number

request.description:
List brands with explicit **sort order**.

Binary fragment: `brands?sortBy=`

**Client symbol:** `apiGenericBrandsShortBy`

### Query parameters
| Param | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `sortBy` | string | Sort field (e.g. `name`, `price`) |
| `page` | integer | Page number |

**Auth:** Bearer token (optional, inherited from collection)

Get **full detail** for a single brand/drug by its ID.

**Client symbol:** `apiDrugsDetailsList`

> ⚠️ Note: `/drugs/{id}` returns **404** — the correct path is `/brands/{id}` (confirmed by live probe).

### Path parameters
| Param | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `brand_id` | integer | Brand/drug ID (snowflake-style large integer from list responses). Collection variable: `{{brand_id}}` |

### Response `data` fields (detail — extended vs list)
Includes full prescribing info, indication list, side effects, dosage, nested `generic`, `company`, `dosageForm`, and `packages` arrays.

**Auth:** Bearer token (optional, inherited from collection)

item.name: ❤️ Favorites
item.description:
User's saved/favorited brands. **Authentication required** for all endpoints in this folder.

**Client symbols (binary fragments):** `favorites/add/`, `favorites/remove/`

> ⚠️ **Evidence level:** Path fragments from `libapp.so`. Naive POST to `/favorites/add` returned **404** in live probing — the brand ID is likely in the URL path as `/favorites/add/{brand_id}` or passed as body `{"brand_id": N}`. Both patterns documented below.

**Prerequisite:** Must call Login first to get `auth_token`.

request.description:
Retrieve the **current user's favorited brands**.

Live probe: `GET /favorites` → **200** (returns token-missing envelope without auth).

### Response `data`
Array of brand objects (same shape as brand detail).

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` ✅ **Required**

**Add a brand to the user's favorites** (path-ID pattern).

Binary fragment: `favorites/add/` — trailing slash in binary suggests the brand ID follows in the URL path.

### Path parameters
| Param | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `brand_id` | integer | Brand/drug ID to favorite. Collection variable: `{{brand_id}}` |

> ⚠️ If this returns 404, try the **Add Favorite (Body-based)** request below.

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` ✅ **Required**

**Alternative pattern** — Add a brand to favorites using a JSON body instead of path ID.

### Request body
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `brand_id` | integer | ID of the brand to add |

> Use this if the path-based variant returns 404.

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` ✅ **Required**

**Remove a brand from the user's favorites** (path-ID pattern).

Binary fragment: `favorites/remove/` — same trailing-slash pattern as `add/`.

### Path parameters
| Param | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `brand_id` | integer | Brand/drug ID to un-favorite. Collection variable: `{{brand_id}}` |

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` ✅ **Required**

item.name: 📜 History
item.description:
User's search/view history. **Authentication required** for all endpoints in this folder.

**Client symbols (binary fragments):** `histories/remove/`, `histories/remove/all`

**Prerequisite:** Must call Login first to get `auth_token`.

request.description:
Retrieve the **current user's search/view history**.

Live probe: `GET /histories` → **200** (token-missing envelope without auth).

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` ✅ **Required**

**Clear all history entries** for the current user.

Live probe: `GET /histories/remove/all` → returns **JSON envelope** (auth required for success).

> Note: Uses **GET** method — confirmed by binary string and live probe behavior.

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` ✅ **Required**

**Remove a single history entry** by ID.

Binary fragment: `histories/remove/` — same pattern as favorites, ID likely in path.

### Path parameters
| Param | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `history_id` | integer | History entry ID to remove. Collection variable: `{{history_id}}` |

> ⚠️ **Evidence level: INFERRED.** HTTP method may be DELETE rather than POST — try both.

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` ✅ **Required**

item.name: 📞 Contact & Misc
item.description: Contact form and medicine request submission endpoints.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "John Doe",
  "email": "john.doe@example.com",
  "subject": "Query about medicine",
  "message": "I would like to know more about your drug database.",
  "phone": "01711943040"
}

request.description:
**Submit a contact form message** to MedEx support.

**Client symbol:** `apiContactUs`

Live probe: `GET /contact` → **405 Method Not Allowed** (confirms POST required). Empty POST → **500** (schema not fully verified).

### Request body fields (inferred from binary strings: `subject`, `message`, `email`)
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | string | Likely ✅ | Sender's name |
| `email` | string | Likely ✅ | Sender's email |
| `subject` | string | Optional | Message subject |
| `message` | string | Likely ✅ | Message body |
| `phone` | string | Optional | Contact phone number |

**Auth:** Bearer token (optional, inherited from collection)

**Request to add a missing medicine** to the MedEx database.

Live probe: `GET /request/add-medicine` → returns **JSON envelope** (auth may be required for the actual action).

> ⚠️ This endpoint may require a POST body in the actual app flow (e.g. medicine name, generic, company). The exact schema is not confirmed from the binary.

**Auth:** `Authorization: Bearer {{auth_token}}` (recommended — inherited from collection)

item.name: 🛒 ACIBD Order System
item.description:
# ACIBD MyACI Order Backend

**Base URL:** `http://apps.acibd.com/apps/myaci/` (**cleartext HTTP — plain HTTP, not HTTPS**)

This is a **separate backend** operated by ACI BD for the integrated ordering feature within MedEx.

## ACIBD Auth Flow
1. **ACIBD Login** → provides `Customer Code` + `Mobile No` → triggers OTP send
2. **ACIBD Send OTP** → sends OTP to mobile
3. Use the session token (`order_token`) for subsequent calls
4. **ACIBD Add Order** — submit an order
5. **ACIBD Product List** — browse available products

## ACIBD Error envelope
```json
{
  "error": ["The Customer Code field is required.", "The OTP field is required.", "The Mobile No field is required."],
  "success": 0,
  "message": "Precondition Failed"
}
```

exec: // Capture ACIBD order token if returned

request.description:
**Log in to the ACIBD MyACI order system.**

**Source:** `libapp.so` URL string + live POST probe (error envelope confirmed)

> ⚠️ **Plain HTTP** — this URL uses `http://` (cleartext). The app has `android:usesCleartextTraffic="true"` in the manifest.

### Request body fields (confirmed by server validation error messages)
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `Customer Code` | string | ✅ Yes | ACIBD customer code |
| `Mobile No` | string | ✅ Yes | Registered mobile number |
| `OTP` | string | ✅ Yes | OTP (leave empty initially — triggers OTP send) |

### Error response (empty POST)
```json
{
  "error": [
```

item.name: 📊 Google Apps Script Webhook
item.description: Internal analytics/logging webhook. Triggered from native `MainActivity.java` via Flutter platform channel `com.medex.bd.medex/sheet` to log user events (e.g., login data) to a Google Sheet.

item.name: Apps Script — Log Event to Sheet

request.description:
**POST to Google Apps Script macro** — logs user/event data to a connected Google Sheet.

**Source:** `com/medex/bd/medex/MainActivity.java` — static method `n(Map)`. Called via Flutter platform channel `com.medex.bd.medex/sheet` → `sendLoginUserToSheetIfLoggedIn`.

### Implementation details (from decompiled Java)
- Builds `JSONObject` from the incoming `Map<String, Object>` — null values are sent as `""`
- HTTP method: **POST**
- Headers set in Java: `Content-Type: application/json; utf-8`, `Accept: application/json`
- App only checks HTTP **200** status; response body is **not parsed**

### Request body
Flat JSON object with whatever key-value pairs the Dart side passes via the channel. Likely contains login/user analytics fields.

**Auth:** None (Google Apps Script public macro URL acts as the secret)
